title: edhoc: ID_CRED_R/I not validated against expected peer pubkey
description:
File: python/src/lichen/crypto/edhoc.py
Lines: 337-345 (process_message_2), 613-620 (process_message_3)

In EdhocInitiator.process_message_2():
- Line 337: id_cred_r = pt2_items[0] - extracted from plaintext_2
- Line 345: cred_r = peer_pubkey - uses the caller-provided pubkey
- Line 348: context_2 includes both id_cred_r and cred_r
- No check that id_cred_r == peer_pubkey

If the responder sends a different ID_CRED_R than their actual pubkey, the MAC_2 computation uses mismatched values. The signature verification uses peer_pubkey but the MAC context uses id_cred_r from the message.

Same issue in EdhocResponder.process_message_3():
- Line 613: id_cred_i extracted from message
- Line 620: cred_i = peer_pubkey

Impact: Could cause interoperability issues or authentication confusion. Per RFC 9528, the receiver should verify ID_CRED matches the expected peer credential.
close_reason: Added ID_CRED validation against peer_pubkey in process_message_2/3 (Python EDHOC). Prevents mismatch per RFC 9528. Commit d1a4add47.
